title: Injection overview
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: Injection vulnerabilities happen when untrusted input is interpreted as part of a command or query. SQL injection is the common example: user input is concatenated into a SQL statement and changes its meaning. Defensive patterns include parameterized queries, strict input validation, output encoding for the target context, least privilege database accounts, and logging suspicious payloads.

title: Broken access control overview
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: Broken access control means a user can perform actions or access objects outside their authorization boundary. Common examples include changing an object id in a URL, calling an admin API without proper checks, or relying only on frontend controls. A secure design enforces authorization on the server for every sensitive operation and uses deny-by-default rules.

title: Cross-site scripting overview
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: Cross-site scripting occurs when attacker-controlled script is executed in another user's browser. Stored XSS is persisted in backend data, reflected XSS is returned immediately from a request, and DOM XSS happens in client-side code. Mitigation depends on context-aware output encoding, safe templating, Content Security Policy, and avoiding dangerous DOM APIs with untrusted data.

title: Zero trust overview
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: Zero trust is a security model that assumes network location alone should not grant trust. Access decisions should consider identity, device posture, resource sensitivity, policy, and continuous verification. For engineering work, this usually means strong identity, least privilege, segmentation, telemetry, and explicit authorization for each request.

title: Credential access overview
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: Credential access techniques aim to steal account names, passwords, tokens, keys, or session material. Defenders should monitor suspicious authentication behavior, protect secrets at rest, avoid plaintext credentials in scripts, rotate exposed credentials, and reduce credential reuse across systems.

title: Known exploited vulnerabilities overview
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: A known exploited vulnerability is a vulnerability observed in real attacks. Security teams should prioritize these issues higher than theoretical vulnerabilities with the same severity score. A practical workflow combines asset inventory, exposure assessment, patch availability, compensating controls, and verification that remediation actually happened.

title: SQL 注入防护概要
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: SQL 注入通常发生在程序把用户输入直接拼接进 SQL 语句时。攻击者可以改变查询条件、读取敏感数据或绕过登录。常见防护包括参数化查询、输入校验、最小权限数据库账号、错误信息收敛、审计日志和 WAF 辅助检测。

title: 零信任概要
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: 零信任不是某个单一产品，而是一种安全架构思路。它不因为请求来自内网就默认可信，而是持续基于身份、设备状态、资源敏感度、访问策略和行为信号做授权判断。工程落地通常包括强身份、最小权限、分段、持续监控和显式授权。

title: 已知被利用漏洞处理概要
license_note: Public learning note. Text is original summary, not copied from source.
text: 已知被利用漏洞是已经在真实攻击中被观察到的漏洞。安全团队应优先处理这类漏洞，而不是只看 CVSS 分数。实践流程包括确认资产是否受影响、判断暴露面、安排补丁或缓解措施、验证修复结果，并记录无法立即修复时的补偿控制。
